answer:
The Google Ads API uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication [1][2]. To make API calls, you must provide a developer token and an OAuth 2.0 access token in your request headers [1][3]. If you are accessing accounts via a manager account, you must also provide a login customer ID header [1][3].

The required headers are [1][3]:
- Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN
- developer-token: DEVELOPER_TOKEN
- login-customer-id: MANAGER_CUSTOMER_ID (required when using a manager account)

```http
GET /v24/customers:listAccessibleCustomers HTTP/1.1
Host: googleads.googleapis.com
Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN
developer-token: DEVELOPER_TOKEN
login-customer-id: MANAGER_CUSTOMER_ID
```

Supported OAuth 2.0 workflows include service account, single-user, and multi-user authentication, all utilizing the scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords` [1][4][2].
